ddls:
CREATE TABLE "titles" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each title */,
  "local_title" TEXT /* The title as it appears locally in the specific market */,
  "original_title" TEXT /* The original title as it was released in its home country */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the title is from */,
  "title_type" TEXT /* Type of title (e.g., Movie, TV Show) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the title */,
  "production_year" INTEGER /* Year of production */,
  "director" TEXT /* Director's name */,
  " runtime" INTEGER /* Movie/Episode runtime in minutes */,
  "age_rating" TEXT /* Age rating for the title (e.g., PG, R, TV-14) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_titles_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Full name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code representing the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the world where the country is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., Drama, Comedy) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "parent_genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent genre (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "title_genres" (
  "title_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the title-genre relationship */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre associated with the title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_title_genres_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "releases" (
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each release */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title being released */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Country where the title is released */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date of the release */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of release (e.g., DVD, Blu-ray, Streaming) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the title (e.g., Director's Cut) */,
  "price" TEXT /* Price of the title */,
  "availability" INTEGER /* Whether the title is currently available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_releases_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "title_variations" (
  "variation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each title variation */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title the variation belongs to */,
  "variation_type" TEXT /* Type of variation (e.g., Localization, Adaptation) */,
  "variation_title" TEXT /* The variation of the title presented */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the variation (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("variation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_title_variations_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., content manager, data analyst) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the title */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cover_art" (
  "art_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cover art entry */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title the cover art is associated with */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the cover art image */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the cover art was uploaded */,
  "image_format" TEXT /* Format of the image (e.g., JPEG, PNG) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("art_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cover_art_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title the note is about */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the note */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* The content of the note */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* When the note was created */,
  "edited_at" TEXT /* When the note was last edited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notes_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user giving the rating */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Rating score (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "review" TEXT /* User review for the title */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date the rating was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title the version belongs to */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version (e.g., Director's Cut) */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Date of birth of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "title_actors" (
  "title_actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each title-actor relationship */,
  "title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the title */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role played by the actor in the title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_title_actors_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("title_id") REFERENCES titles ("title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_title_actors_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id")
)
